description.en: Apple Scab is caused by the fungus Venturia inaequalis. It creates dark, scaly lesions on leaves and fruit, reducing yield and fruit quality.

en: Dark, scaly lesions on leaves and fruit

en.title: Neem Oil Spray
en.description: Mix 30ml neem oil per liter of water, spray every 7-10 days

en.title: Lime Sulfur
en.description: Apply before bud break each spring season

en.description: Prune and burn all affected branches and leaves

en.title: Mancozeb 75 WP

en.title: Myclobutanil
en.description: Systemic fungicide, apply at first sign of disease

description.en: Apple Black Rot is a serious fungal disease that affects fruit, leaves and branches. Fruit becomes black and mummified, causing major crop loss.

en: Brown circular spots with purple borders on leaves
Cankers on branches with reddish-brown margins

en.title: Remove Mummies
en.description: Pick and destroy all mummified fruit on tree and ground

en.title: Bordeaux Mixture
en.description: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture (copper sulfate + lime) at dormant stage

en.description: Remove and burn all cankered branches at least 15cm below visible infection

en.description: Spray 2g/liter every 10-14 days during season

en.description: Apply 1g/liter as systemic treatment

en.title: Ziram 76 WG
en.description: Apply as protective spray at pink bud stage

description.en: Cedar Apple Rust is a fungal disease requiring two host plants - apple and cedar/juniper trees. It creates bright orange-yellow spots on apple leaves and fruit.

en: Bright yellow-orange spots on upper leaf surface
Orange tube-like structures on leaf undersides
Requires cedar/juniper trees nearby to complete life cycle
Remove nearby cedar or juniper trees if possible
Spray fungicides at pink bud stage

en.description: Apply wettable sulfur 3g/liter starting at pink bud stage

en.title: Neem Oil
en.description: Spray 30ml neem oil per liter every 7-10 days

en.description: Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees to break disease cycle

en.description: Apply 1ml/liter at pink bud stage, repeat at petal fall

en.title: Propiconazole
en.description: Spray 1ml/liter every 10 days from early spring

en.description: Apply 2.5g/liter as protective cover spray

description.en: Gray Leaf Spot (Cercospora) is one of the most damaging maize diseases in India. It causes rectangular gray/tan lesions that can destroy entire leaves, reducing yield by 50% or more.

Severe cases cause entire leaf death
Crop rotation - avoid maize after maize

description.en: Common Rust of Maize causes golden-brown powdery pustules on both leaf surfaces. It can reduce photosynthesis and yield if it spreads widely.

en: Golden-brown to brick-red oval pustules on leaves
Pustules on both upper and lower leaf surface
Leaves turn yellow and die in severe cases
Powdery rust spores visible when rubbed
Early planting to avoid peak rust season
Monitor fields regularly from seedling stage

description.en: Northern Leaf Blight causes large cigar-shaped gray-green lesions on maize leaves. It is one of the most serious maize foliar diseases in India.

Remove infected crop debris after harvest

description.en: Grape Black Rot causes brown lesions on leaves and turns fruit into hard, black mummified berries. It is a major disease of grapes in India.

en: Small brown circular spots on leaves with dark borders
Infected berries shrivel and turn black
Hard mummified berries remain on cluster
Brown lesions on shoots and tendrils
Rain and heavy dew during growing season
Remove all mummified berries from vine
Apply fungicides before and after rain

description.en: Esca or Black Measles is a serious wood disease of grapevines. It causes striped discoloration of leaves and dark spots on berries, eventually killing the vine.

en: Tiger-stripe pattern on leaves - yellow/red between green veins
Dark spots with lighter halos on berries
Sudden vine collapse in severe cases
White rot inside trunk when cut
Seal pruning wounds with wound protectant paste

en.description: Cut out infected wood well below diseased area

description.en: Citrus Greening (Huanglongbing/HLB) is the most destructive citrus disease in the world. There is no cure - infected trees must be removed. It causes lopsided, bitter fruit and eventually kills the tree.

en: Yellow mottling on leaves - asymmetric unlike deficiency
Tree decline and death over 5-8 years
Spread by Asian Citrus Psyllid insect
Control citrus psyllid vector with insecticides
Remove and destroy infected trees immediately

en.description: Remove and destroy all infected trees - there is no cure

description.en: Bacterial Spot causes water-soaked spots on peach leaves and fruit. In severe cases it causes major defoliation and fruit defects, significantly reducing marketable yield.

en: Small water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown
Spots may fall out leaving shot-hole appearance
Premature fruit drop in severe cases
Wounds from insects or hail allow entry
Avoid working in orchard when wet
Apply copper sprays from dormant season

en: Small water-soaked spots on leaves turning brown
Severe defoliation exposing fruit to sunscald
Rain splash and insect movement spread bacteria

Rotate crops - avoid pepper/tomato for 2 years

description.en: Late Blight is the most devastating potato disease - it caused the Irish Famine. In India, it can destroy an entire crop in days under favorable conditions. Immediate action is critical.

en: Water-soaked dark lesions on leaf edges and tips
White fuzzy mold on leaf underside in humid weather
Infected tubers show reddish-brown rot inside
Entire field can collapse within 7-10 days
Infected seed tubers are primary source
Plant resistant varieties (Kufri Jyoti, Kufri Badshah)
Earthing up tubers reduces tuber infection

en.title: Bordeaux Mixture
en.description: URGENT: Spray 1% Bordeaux mixture immediately - 10g copper sulfate + 10g lime per liter

Tiny moving dots visible with magnifying glass
Maintain proper irrigation to reduce plant stress
Avoid broad-spectrum pesticides that kill predatory mites

description.en: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (TYLCV) is spread by whitefly insects. It causes severe yellowing, curling of leaves and stunting, with major yield loss. There is no cure - prevention is key.

en: Upward curling and yellowing of leaves
Severely infected plants produce little or no fruit
Spread by silverleaf whitefly Bemisia tabaci
No cure - must control vector insect
Control whitefly with yellow sticky traps
Use reflective mulch to deter whiteflies
Plant resistant varieties (Arka Rakshak, Pusa Rohini)
Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Plant barrier crops like maize around tomato field

en: Mosaic pattern of light and dark green on leaves
Necrotic streaks on stems in some strains
Spread by contact - hands, tools, clothing
Wash hands with soap before handling plants
Avoid tobacco - workers who smoke can spread TMV

en.description: Plant ToMV-resistant varieties like Arka Abhijit, Arka Saurabh
